title: messages: FileHeader.mime_type has no length bound
description: FileHeader.mime_type is a plain String with no length limit enforced at deserialization. A hostile peer can send a multi-megabyte MIME type string, causing unbounded heap allocation on any receiver that deserializes ClearMessage. The other FileHeader fields (name, sha256_hex, total_chunks) are already bounded; mime_type was missed. Expected fix: add deserialize_mime_type limiting the field to e.g. 256 bytes (longest real MIME types are under 100 chars).
